uk: Свв. мучеників Мини, Єрмогена і Євграфа.

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 Октоїха, 3 мчч., Сл., І нині: богород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мчч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мчч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар мчч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мчч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і мчч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний мчч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря мчч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мчч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – мчч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – Євр. 315 зач.; 7, 1-6. Єв. – Лк. 107 зач.; 21, 28-33.

troparion_en.text: Having slain the fiery ragings and fierce movements of the passions through their ascetic labors, / the Martyrs of Christ, Menas, Hermogenes, and Eugraphus, received grace to dispel the afflictions of the infirm and to work wonders both while alive and after death. / It is a truly great wonder that bare bones pour forth healing. / Glory to our only God and Creator.

kontakion_en.text: The Lord snatched you from the temporal army making you a fellow inheritor of the eternal, O Menas; / together with Hermogenes and Eugraphus with whom you suffered, / you are granted an incorruptible crown.

matched_oca_saint: Martyrs Menas, Hermogenes, and Eugraphus, of Alexandria

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Menas, Hermogenes, and Eugraphus, of Alexandria

troparion.text: Having slain the fiery ragings and fierce movements of the passions through their ascetic labors, / the Martyrs of Christ, Menas, Hermogenes, and Eugraphus, received grace to dispel the afflictions of the infirm and to work wonders both while alive and after death. / It is a truly great wonder that bare bones pour forth healing. / Glory to our only God and Creator.

kontakion.text: The Lord snatched you from the temporal army making you a fellow inheritor of the eternal, O Menas; / together with Hermogenes and Eugraphus with whom you suffered, / you are granted an incorruptible crown.

troparion.text: O divinely-chosen Hierarch of Christ, Ioasaph,  / from childhood you were an extraordinary man of prayer.  / By your pious life, you appeared as a rule of faith and an example of mercy;  / and to those who come to you with faith, you gush forth abundant healing.  / Pray to Christ God that He may confirm our land in Orthodoxy, peace and piety, and save our souls.

kontakion.text: Who can tell of the various struggles of your life? /  Who can count the various mercies of God which you manifested?  / Knowing well your boldness before the Most Pure Theotokos and the all-generous God, we call upon you with compunctionate hearts: /  "Do not deprive us of your help and intercession, O Hierarch of Christ and wonderworker Ioasaph."

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Gemellus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Gemellus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Thomas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name:
Righteous King John of Serbia, and his parents Righteous Stephen, Despot of Serbia, and Venerable
Angelina of Serbia

troparion.text: In you, O Mother, was preserved with exactness what was according to the image; /  for you took up the Cross and followed Christ.  /  By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away;  / but to care for the soul, since it is immortal.  /  Therefore, O Venerable Angelina, your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O victorious Leader...”)
Come, let us praise John,  /  the newly glorified servant of God’s grace,  /  the glory of our Faith and our intercessor / who invites us to sing with grateful voices,   /  and to glorify Him Who glorifies His Saints, /  for he prays for those who honor his memory  /  and who sing to him: “Rejoice, O newly blossomed flower John.”

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Your tomb, O Savior...”)
You shone forth in asceticism, like gold in the furnace, with your divine teachings,  /  you enlightened the choirs of monastics who followed you, O God-bearer Leóntios,  /  and today, as we celebrate your radiant memory,  /  we ask you to intercede with the Lord for us.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
Having divinely armed yourself with the purity of your soul,  / and firmly grasping unceasing prayer as a spear, you pierced the armies of demons.  / Therefore, we beseech you, O our Father Leontius,  / always intercede for us who honor you.
